args.query: Multi-Query Attention vs Grouped-Query Attention LLM inference KV-cache trade-offs

args.report:
# Comparative Report: Multi-Query Attention (MQA) vs. Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) for LLM Inference Optimization

## 1. Executive Summary
Autoregressive inference in Large Language Models (LLMs) is heavily bottlenecked by memory bandwidth, specifically due to the scaling of the Key-Value (KV) cache. Multi-Query Attention (MQA) and Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) are key architectural optimizations designed to alleviate this bottleneck by reducing the memory footprint of the KV cache. While MQA provides maximum memory savings at the cost of potential quality degradation, GQA serves as a parameterized generalization that interpolates between Multi-Head Attention (MHA) and MQA, delivering near-MHA quality alongside near-MQA inference speeds.

---

## 2. Architectural Comparison

| Feature | Multi-Head Attention (MHA) | Multi-Query Attention (MQA) | Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **KV Head Mapping** | $H$ Query heads map to $H$ unique Key & Value heads (1:1 relationship). | All $H$ Query heads share 1 single Key & Value head ($H$:1 relationship). | $H$ Query heads are grouped into $G$ groups. Each group shares 1 KV head ($H/G$:1 relationship). |
| **KV Cache Size** | Large ($O(\text{Batch} \times \text{SeqLen} \times H \times D_k)$). | Minimal ($O(\text{Batch} \times \text{SeqLen} \times 1 \times D_k)$). | Moderate/Optimized ($O(\text{Batch} \times \text{SeqLen} \times G \times D_k)$). |
| **Memory Bandwidth Demand** | Very High (Requires loading unique KV vectors for every single head). | Extremely Low (Loads only one shared KV pair per tensor block). | Low/Configurable (Loads $G$ KV pairs, balancing cache access layout). |

Where:
* $H$ = number of query heads
* $G$ = number of grouped key-value heads ($1 < G < H$)
* $D_k$ = dimension of the key/value heads

---

## 3. Memory vs. Quality Trade-Offs (Empirical Evidence)

In the GQA paper (Ainslie et al., 2023), the authors evaluated these mechanisms using a **T5-XXL** (11B parameters) model checkpoint. Rather than training models from scratch, they introduced an **uptraining** method (using 5% of the original pre-training compute) to convert MHA checkpoints into MQA and GQA variants. 

### Table 1: Empirical Evaluation of T5-XXL (Ainslie et al., 2023)
| Attention Configuration | Inference Time (s/sample) | Speed Gain (vs. MHA) | Average Evaluation Performance (ROUGE-1) | Quality Delta (vs. MHA) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **MHA Baseline** | 1.51s | 1.0× | 47.2 | -- |
| **MQA** | 0.24s | 6.3× | 46.6 | -0.6 |
| **GQA-8** (8 KV heads) | 0.28s | 5.4× | 47.1 | -0.1 |

### Analysis of Trade-offs:
1. **Multi-Query Attention (MQA) Trade-offs:**
   * **Advantages**: Delivers the highest inference throughput boost (6.3× speedup) and the lowest memory footprint. It minimizes the memory bandwidth bottleneck, enabling significantly larger batch sizes and context lengths.
   * **Disadvantages**: Suffers from noticeable quality degradation (-0.6 drop in average ROUGE-1 score) due to the severe bottleneck of forcing all query heads to share a single representation. It also exhibits higher training instability when trained from scratch.
   
2. **Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) Trade-offs:**
   * **Advantages**: Almost completely recovers the quality loss of MQA, trailing the original MHA baseline by only a nominal margin (-0.1 average ROUGE-1), while retaining 85%+ of MQA's speedup (5.4× vs. 6.3×).
   * **Disadvantages**: Introduces a slightly larger memory footprint compared to MQA, proportional to the group division factor $G$. For example, a GQA-8 model uses 8 times more KV cache memory than MQA, though still $H/8$ times less than MHA.

---

## 4. Modern Industry Adoption
Because GQA offers a superior Pareto-optimal trade-off than either pure MHA or pure MQA, it has become the de facto standard in state-of-the-art LLMs, facilitating massive context windows (up to 128k or more) on standard hardware:
* **Llama 2 (70B only) & Llama 3 (All sizes)**: Transitioned from MHA/MQA configurations to GQA (specifically utilizing 8 key-value heads) to support high-throughput, long-context serving.
* **Mistral 7B & Mixtral 8x7B**: Standardized on GQA to allow higher batch sizes and lower memory consumption during local consumer-GPU deployment.
* **Qwen 2 / Gemma 2**: Integrate GQA variant formats to optimize parameter-to-throughput efficiency during served inference API contexts.
